rulings.Case Title: BRIAN CULLEN VS. SAN FRANCISCO GRAVEL CO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Cross-Defendants Valley Excavating And C And R Trucking, Inc'S Motion For Determination Of Good Faith Settlement
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar on Friday, March 14, 2014, Line 2, Cross-Defendants Valley Excavating And C And R Trucking, Inc's Motion For Determination Of Good Faith Settlement (part 1/2 of tentative ruling). Motion Denied. C and R Trucking and Valley Excavating (collectively "C&R") did not proffer any evidence regarding probable liability, but instead merely repeated multiple times that it admits no fault or liability and contends that its activities did not contribute to the alleged damages. It supports these contentions only with the Rouhier declaration, which has been ruled inadmissible (see below). Moreover, the figure of $20,000 was calculated as "roughly" 10% of Hughes' alleged damages, and C&R claims that this is in the "ballpark" because it is "merely buying its peace" since it admits no liability, and provides no further evidence to suggest that this is a reasonable and proportionate approximation of liability. In the absence of any substantial evidence to show the $20,000 settlement amount was within the reasonable range of C&R's proportionate liability, SFG met its burden in attacking the settlement. San Francisco Gravel's (SFG) objections both granted. Expert opinions are purely conclusory if they are not accompanied by a reasoned explanation that connects the factual predicates to the ultimate conclusion and thus have no evidentiary value. Jennings v. Palomar Pomerado Health Systems, Inc. (2003) 114 Cal.App.4th 1108, 1117. Here, Rouhier's declaration states that the rust stains were caused by marcasite in the sand and/or contaminated sand in a finish coat system not produced, transported, provided, or sold by any entity in this case. In support he points only to his review of the homes, documents and photographs, and states that he is not aware of any testing that would determine whether sand contains such contaminants. This opinion does not provide a clear conclusion of causation, as it could be the marcasite or the finish coat system, and Rouhier does not explain the difference between them or how either could not have resulted from any action taken by C&R. Second, there are no facts detailing what in the review of the homes and documents led to the opinion, and there is no connection between Rouhier's asserted expertise and the conclusions. Thus, the opinion is conclusory and lacks an adequate foundation. Tentative ruling continued- see part 2/2. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: BRIAN CULLEN VS. SAN FRANCISCO GRAVEL CO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Cross-Defendants Valley Excavating And C And R Trucking, Inc'S Motion For Determination Of Good Faith Settlement (Part 2/2 Of Tentative Ruling).
rulings.Rulings: Cross-Defendants Valley Excavating And C And R Trucking, Inc'S Motion For Determination Of Good Faith Settlement (Part 2/2 Of Tentative Ruling). Moreover, "[i]t is thoroughly established that experts may not give opinions on matters which are essentially within the province of the court to decide." Sheldon Appel Co. v. Albert & Oliker (1989) 47 Cal.3d 863, 884. That is, expert testimony may not contain legal conclusions. Summers v. A.L. Gilbert Co. (1999) 69 C.A.4th 1155. Here, the liability of the entities is a question of law. As such, Rouhier's conclusion that "any issue with contaminated sand would not be the responsibility of any entity that requested a certain aggregate size for sale" is a prohibited legal conclusion. Cf. Ecco-Phoenix Electric Corp. v. Howard J. White, Inc. (1969) 1 Cal.3d 266, 271 (court properly sustained objections to "who should pay" hypotheticals, but permitted questions as to the existence of custom and usage related to payment in the expert's particular industry). Thus, the assertion is not accompanied by any rationale, any evidence of custom, or any other basis for forming the conclusion. Moreover, the declaration does not detail Rouhier's expertise, mentioning only that he is a licensed general contractor in California and a principal with RH Building Contractors, and his CV (Exhibit A) is not included. Absent explication on his qualifications, and a factual-or any-basis for this conclusion, D's objection is sustained. rulings.Case Title: BRIAN MCVEIGH VS RECOLOGY, ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Depositions At Deft Production Of Documents And Request For Sanctions,
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Friday, March 14, 2014, Line 1, PLAINTIFF BRIAN MCVEIGH's Motion To Compel Depositions At Deft Production Of Documents And Request For Sanctions Pro Tem Judge Katherine Huibonhoa, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem has not issued a tentative ruling on this motion. Each party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing.

rulings.Case Title: NORA GONG VS. HING LUNG COMPANY, et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar on Friday, March 14, 2014, Line 11, DEFENDANTs HING LUNG COMPANY, CHUCK GONG, KATHY GONG and YUET GONG's DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Defendants' general demurrer to the First Amended Complaint as time-barred is overruled. Defendants' demurrer to the first cause of action for breach of contract and second cause of action for specific performance is sustained with leave to amend to address the factual deficiencies as related to any joint tenancy terms, Defendants' alleged breaches, and the resulting damages sustained by the Plaintiff. The Defendants' demurrer to the third cause of action for accounting is sustained without leave to amend because the Plaintiff does not plead the existence of a limited partnership under Corporation Code section 15902.01 and the existence of a partnership is a prerequisite for any accounting cause of action pursuant to Corporation Code section 15910.01(a). The Defendants' demurrer to the fourth cause of action for declaratory relief is sustained with leave to amend because declaratory relief is unnecessary under Code of Civil Procedure section 1061 until the Plaintiff adequately pleads a breach of contract cause of action. The Defendants' demurrer to the fifth cause of action for violation of Business and Professions Code section 17200 is sustained with leave to amend because the First Amended Complaint fails to plead with particularity the facts supporting the violation. Defendants' motion to strike the Plaintiff's Opposition for Untimely Filing and Service is denied. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: SHERRY FREIMAN VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Verified Responses To Form Interrogatories And Verified Responses To Request For Production Of Documents And Produce Responsive Documents; And For Further Verified Responses To Special Interrogatories, Set 1
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Friday, March 14, 2014, Line 4, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO's Mtn To Compel Verified Responses To Form Interrogatories And Verified Responses To Request For Production Of Documents And Produce Responsive Documents; And For Further Verified Responses To Special Interrogatories, Set 1 Pro Tem Judge Katherine Huibonhoa, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Granted, for reasons set forth in defendant City and County of San Francisco¿s moving papers. There is no record that plaintiff Sherry Freiman opposes this motion. It is hereby ordered that within fifteen (15) days of the notice of entry of the signed Order on this motion: (1) plaintiff Freiman shall provide verified responses to Form Interrogatories, Set No. 1; (2) plaintiff Freiman shall provide verified responses to Special Interrogatories, Set No. 1; (3) plaintiff Freiman shall provide verified responses to defendant¿s Request For Production of Documents, Set No. 1, and produce all responsive documents, The Court further awards monetary sanctions against plaintiff Freiman in the amount of $500.00, to be paid within fifteen (15) days of the notice of entry of the signed Order on this motion. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to katherinehuibonhoa@paulhastings.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested.

rulings.Case Title: CHRISTINA NICHOLAS VS. GREENE MOTORS, INC. DBA AVERY GREENE HONDA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Joinder In Petition To Compel Binding Arb And Stay Action
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar on Friday, March 14, 2014, Line 13, DEFENDANT AMERICAN HONDA MOTOR CO., INC.'s Joinder In Petition To Compel Binding Arbitration And Stay Action. Granted. The action is stayed pending the arbitration before the American Arbitration Association or mutually agreed upon alternative forum. Plaintiff has not met her burden to demonstrate that the arbitration provision is unconscionable. Plaintiff's interpretation of the arbitration clause would give her unilateral authority to choose the arbitration forum, and the arbitration provision is not void as illusory. Although there is minimal evidence of procedural unconscionability, Plaintiff has not demonstrated the high degree of substantive unconscionability necessary to render the arbitration provision unenforceable. The arbitration provision provides that Greene Motors, Inc. shall pay the first $ 5000 of Plaintiff's arbitration costs. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: FRANK CHU VS. YARA JIANG et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel Answers To Form Interrogatories
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Friday, March 14, 2014, Line 5, DEFENDANT YARA JIANG'S Motion To Compel Answers To Form Interrogatories Pro Tem Judge Katherine Huibonhoa, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Granted, for reasons set forth in defendant Yara Jiang¿s moving papers. There is no record that plaintiff Frank Chu opposes this motion. It is hereby ordered that within fifteen (15) days of the signed Order on this motion: (1) plaintiff Chu shall provide verified responses, without objection, to Form Interrogatories, Set No. 1; (2) plaintiff Chu shall provide verified responses, without objection, to Special Interrogatories, Set No. 1; (3) plaintiff Chu shall provide verified responses, without objection, to defendant¿s Demand for Production, Set No. 1, and produce all responsive documents, The Court further awards monetary sanctions against plaintiff Chu in the amount of $420.00, to be paid within fifteen (15) days of the signed Order on this motion. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to katherinehuibonhoa@paulhastings.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/jpt
